title: Exercises: Kraus Operators
description: Practice implementing and analyzing Kraus operator representations

exercises.title: Implement Standard Channels
exercises.description: Implement Kraus operators for depolarizing, amplitude damping, and phase damping channels. Verify the completeness relation.
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def depolarizing_kraus(p):
    """Kraus operators for depolarizing channel."""
    I = np.eye(2)
    X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
    Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]])
    Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]])
    return [
        np.sqrt(1-p) * I,
        np.sqrt(p/3) * X,
        np.sqrt(p/3) * Y,
        np.sqrt(p/3) * Z
    ]

def amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma):
    """Kraus operators for amplitude damping."""
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]])
    return [K0, K1]

def phase_damping_kraus(lambda_):
    """Kraus operators for phase damping."""
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-lambda_)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, np.sqrt(lambda_)]])
    return [K0, K1]

def verify_completeness(kraus_ops):
    """Verify sum K^dag K = I."""
    dim = kraus_ops[0].shape[0]
    total = sum(K.conj().T @ K for K in kraus_ops)
    return np.allclose(total, np.eye(dim))

# Test all channels
print("Depolarizing (p=0.2):", verify_completeness(depolarizing_kraus(0.2)))
print("Amplitude damping (gamma=0.3):", verify_completeness(amplitude_damping_kraus(0.3)))
print("Phase damping (lambda=0.5):", verify_completeness(phase_damping_kraus(0.5)))
hints: Pauli matrices: X flips, Y rotates with phase, Z is diagonal

exercises.title: Channel Composition
exercises.description: Implement channel composition via Kraus operators. Show that composing two amplitude damping channels gives an effective damping rate.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def compose_kraus(kraus1, kraus2):
    """Compose channels: E2 o E1."""
    pass

def effective_ad_gamma(gamma1, gamma2):
    """Theoretical effective gamma for composed AD."""
    pass

# Verify composition formula
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma):
    K0 = np.array([[1, 0], [0, np.sqrt(1-gamma)]])
    K1 = np.array([[0, np.sqrt(gamma)], [0, 0]])
    return [K0, K1]

def compose_kraus(kraus1, kraus2):
    """Compose channels: E2 o E1."""
    return [L @ K for L in kraus2 for K in kraus1]

def apply_channel(rho, kraus_ops):
    return sum(K @ rho @ K.conj().T for K in kraus_ops)

def effective_ad_gamma(gamma1, gamma2):
    """Effective gamma = 1 - (1-g1)(1-g2)."""
    return gamma1 + gamma2 - gamma1 * gamma2

# Test composition
gamma1, gamma2 = 0.2, 0.3
kraus1 = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma1)
kraus2 = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma2)
composed = compose_kraus(kraus1, kraus2)

# Apply to |1><1|
rho1 = np.array([[0, 0], [0, 1]], dtype=complex)
output_composed = apply_channel(rho1, composed)

# Compare with single AD at effective gamma
gamma_eff = effective_ad_gamma(gamma1, gamma2)
kraus_eff = amplitude_damping_kraus(gamma_eff)
output_eff = apply_channel(rho1, kraus_eff)

print(f"Composed output:\n{output_composed}")
print(f"Effective AD output:\n{output_eff}")
print(f"Match: {np.allclose(output_composed, output_eff)}")
print(f"Effective gamma: {gamma_eff:.4f}")

exercises.title: Extract Kraus from Choi
exercises.description: Extract Kraus operators from a Choi matrix via eigendecomposition. Verify they reproduce the original channel.
exercises.starterCode:
import numpy as np

def choi_to_kraus(choi, dim):
    """Extract Kraus operators from Choi matrix."""
    pass

def kraus_to_choi(kraus_ops, dim):
    """Compute Choi matrix from Kraus operators."""
    pass

# Round-trip test
exercises.solution:
import numpy as np

def choi_to_kraus(choi, dim):
    """Extract Kraus operators from Choi matrix."""
    eigvals, eigvecs = np.linalg.eigh(choi)
    kraus_ops = []
    for val, vec in zip(eigvals, eigvecs.T):
        if val > 1e-10:
            # Reshape: Choi vec -> Kraus operator
            K = np.sqrt(val * dim) * vec.reshape(dim, dim, order='F')
            kraus_ops.append(K)
    return kraus_ops

def kraus_to_choi(kraus_ops, dim):
    """Compute Choi matrix from Kraus operators."""
    choi = np.zeros((dim**2, dim**2), dtype=complex)
    for K in kraus_ops:
        # |K>> = vec(K) in column-major order
        K_vec = K.flatten(order='F')
        choi += np.outer(K_vec, K_vec.conj())
    return choi / dim

def apply_channel(rho, kraus_ops):
    return sum(K @ rho @ K.conj().T for K in kraus_ops)

# Test with depolarizing channel
def depolarizing_kraus(p):
    I = np.eye(2)
    X = np.array([[0, 1], [1, 0]])
    Y = np.array([[0, -1j], [1j, 0]])
    Z = np.array([[1, 0], [0, -1]])
    return [np.sqrt(1-p)*I, np.sqrt(p/3)*X, np.sqrt(p/3)*Y, np.sqrt(p/3)*Z]

# Original Kraus
p = 0.2
original_kraus = depolarizing_kraus(p)

# Convert to Choi and back
choi = kraus_to_choi(original_kraus, 2)
recovered_kraus = choi_to_kraus(choi, 2)

print(f"Original Kraus count: {len(original_kraus)}")
print(f"Recovered Kraus count: {len(recovered_kraus)}")

# Verify same channel action
rho = np.array([[0.6, 0.4], [0.4, 0.4]], dtype=complex)
out1 = apply_channel(rho, original_kraus)
out2 = apply_channel(rho, recovered_kraus)
print(f"Same output: {np.allclose(out1, out2)}")
hints: Choi eigendecomposition gives Kraus as reshaped eigenvectors
